AB.plain: The catch-all accounting document type. Unlike every other type, AB can post to all account types — customers, vendors and G/L — which is why it turns up on adjustments and clearing postings.

CO.plain: A document created by Controlling rather than entered in Financial Accounting — cost allocations, assessments and settlements flowing back into the ledger.

DG.plain: Money credited back to a customer — the sales-side reversal of an invoice, reducing what they owe you.

DR.plain: A billing document posted to accounts receivable — what the customer owes you. The FI side of an SD billing document.

DZ.plain: An incoming payment from a customer, normally posted by the payment or bank-statement run rather than typed in by hand.

KG.plain: Money credited back by a vendor — the purchasing-side reversal of an invoice, reducing what you owe them.

KN.plain: A vendor invoice posted under the net procedure, where the cash discount is deducted and posted at invoice time rather than when payment is made.
KN.sap: Vendor net invoice and credit memo

KR.plain: A supplier invoice posted to accounts payable — what you owe. The single most common document type on the purchasing side.

KZ.plain: An outgoing payment to a vendor, usually generated by the automatic payment run (F110) rather than entered manually.

SA.plain: A plain journal entry hitting G/L accounts only — accruals, reclassifications, manual corrections. No customer or vendor involved.
